meta.description: Assesses whether a medical research topic is worth starting now by separating true novelty from pseudo-novelty, auditing real feasibility under stated resource constraints, and forcing a concrete start / narrow / redesign / stop decision. Always requires explicit assumptions and never fabricates references, datasets, resource availability, precedent studies, or publication claims.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, dataset names, statistical values, or clinical data detected; literature-and-resource-integrity-rules enforce explicit uncertainty labeling when verification is not possible.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or unapproved treatment recommendations produced; skill explicitly redirects patient-specific clinical decision requests.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies detected; pseudo-novelty rules, feasibility burden framework, and decision-band rules enforce principled assessment methodology.

code_usability.detail: Mode A, no code generated; Category 1 Evidence Insight skill only.

functional_suitability.note: 15 hard rules, 8 execution steps, 11 mandatory output sections (A–K), and 5 novelty dimensions with explicit pseudo-novelty rejection rules provide complete coverage of novelty, feasibility, precedent, decision-forcing, and self-critical audit tasks.

reliability.note: Forced one-band decision output prevents vague non-committal assessments; however, novelty and saturation claims from training knowledge are not required to carry explicit uncertainty labels, creating a reliability gap for fast-evolving fields.

performance_context.note: 300-line SKILL.md with 14 reference files is on the high end; only 5 of 14 reference files are directly named in SKILL.md, creating orphaned references that may not be used by Claude without explicit pointers.

agent_usability.note: Sample triggers, explicit input validation, 5 valid input formats, and scope redirect template are strong; minor gap in disambiguation guidance when user provides a vague proposal requiring scope narrowing.

human_usability.note: Scope redirect template and 4 sample triggers provide good discoverability; one-band decision output is highly actionable. Minor gap: no guidance on what to do after a 'narrow' or 'redesign' decision.

security.note: Hard rules 11–13 prohibit fabrication of all reference types (PMIDs, DOIs, dataset names, cohort availability, assay access, software access, publication precedent); no credential or prompt injection risks in Mode A.

maintainability.note: 5 of 14 reference files explicitly named in SKILL.md; the remaining 9 (novelty-assessment-framework.md, feasibility-dimension-checklist.md, pseudo-novelty-rejection-rules.md, publication-potential-and-value-rules.md, literature-integrity-rules.md, workflow-step-template.md, output-section-guidance.md, resource-and-dependency-burden-rules.md, decision-band-rules.md) are either sub-referenced internally or orphaned — a P2 maintainability issue.

agent_specific.note: Decision-forcing output (one explicit band, with comparison to adjacent band) is a unique and highly actionable deliverable; minimal executable version template prevents abandonment of borderline projects. Composability interface for downstream skills (e.g., protocol design) not defined.

inputs.label: Spatial transcriptomics of immunotherapy resistance in HCC with small institutional cohort
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. All 11 output sections produced; novelty audit correctly separated 5 dimensions; feasibility audit rated under actual cohort size (n=15) constraint.

assertions.text: Novelty audit covers all 5 dimensions separately (question, context, method, integration, translation novelty)
assertions.note: All 5 novelty dimensions explicitly addressed with separate verdicts.

assertions.text: Feasibility audit includes data access, method burden, validation burden, and timeline burden rated under user's actual constraints
assertions.note: Cohort of n=15 flagged as underpowered; feasibility rated under that specific constraint.

assertions.text: Pseudo-novelty risk explicitly identified (spatial tx + HCC is a crowded emerging space; 'same question in a new tissue' risk flagged)
assertions.note: Pseudo-novelty flag raised with specific rationale.

assertions.text: Minimal executable version (MEV) constructed with narrower question and minimum data requirements
assertions.note: MEV defined with smallest defensible question and minimum cohort threshold.

assertions.text: Final decision resolves to one explicit band with comparison to adjacent band explaining why that band is superior
assertions.note: One primary decision band assigned; adjacent band comparison provided.

inputs.label: Multi-omics sepsis prognosis biomarker project — 6-month timeline, public data only
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Public-data-only constraint properly applied throughout; pseudo-novelty correctly raised for multi-omics + sepsis prognosis as a crowded space.

assertions.text: Pseudo-novelty risk raised for multi-omics + sepsis prognosis as a crowded space requiring clear differentiation rationale
assertions.note: Pseudo-novelty flag raised; skill correctly demanded the differentiating element.

assertions.text: Feasibility assessed specifically under public-data-only constraint, not under ideal hypothetical conditions
assertions.note: Hard Rule 8 applied: feasibility rated under stated resource conditions.

assertions.text: 6-month timeline burden explicitly assessed and matched against actual project scope
assertions.note: Timeline burden rated as a separate feasibility dimension.

assertions.text: No fabricated dataset names or GEO accession availability claims
assertions.note: Literature-and-resource-integrity-rules applied; no invented dataset identifiers.

assertions.text: Decision band explicitly resolves to one of the five named options rather than a vague 'it depends' conclusion
assertions.note: One band assigned with rationale.

inputs.label: BRCA biomarker prediction using ML on methylation data — genuinely novel or just another model paper?
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Hard Rule 7 correctly applied; distinction between interesting topic and good project drawn explicitly.

assertions.text: Method novelty vs. pseudo-novelty separated: ML + methylation combination assessed for scientific gain, not just technical complexity
assertions.note: Hard Rule 3 applied: technical complexity not conflated with scientific novelty.

assertions.text: Hard Rule 7 applied: 'first to combine X and Y' claim challenged unless scientific gain is clear
assertions.note: First-combination claim not treated as sufficient novelty without differentiating scientific rationale.

assertions.text: Precedent and crowding check produced without fabricating specific precedent papers or PMIDs
assertions.note: Literature integrity maintained; field state described with uncertainty labels where unverifiable.

assertions.text: Distinction between 'interesting topic' and 'good project to start now' explicitly drawn
assertions.note: Step 5 judgment present as a standalone assessment.

assertions.text: Self-critical launch audit (Section J) includes strongest reason not to start
assertions.note: Strongest counter-argument to the recommendation explicitly stated.

inputs.label: Extremely vague proposal: studying cancer with AI
inputs.note: 4/5 assertions passed. Vague proposal correctly restated into a specific operational project idea before evaluation; however the restated version was not offered back to the user for confirmation before proceeding.

assertions.text: Vague proposal restated into one specific operational project idea before evaluation begins
assertions.note: Step 1 correctly triggered; vague input converted to a specific operationalized project.

assertions.text: Assumptions underlying the restatement explicitly listed and attributed to skill interpretation
assertions.note: Assumptions stated before assessment proceeds.

assertions.text: Novelty and feasibility audit still produced for the restated proposal
assertions.note: Full assessment produced on the restated version.

assertions.text: Skill pauses to offer user the opportunity to confirm or redirect the restated project scope before full assessment commits
assertions.note: Skill proceeded directly to full assessment without offering the restated scope for user confirmation — risks assessing the wrong project if the restatement was incorrect.

assertions.text: Decision band assigned even for vague input after scope is operationalized
assertions.note: One band assigned for the restated project.

inputs.label: Complex multi-modal project (spatial tx + ChIP-seq + clinical outcomes) with no collaborators, no budget, 3-month timeline
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. All feasibility dimensions assessed under extreme constraints; dependency-heavy components flagged; MEV stripped to minimum; decision band correctly reflects infeasibility of original form.

assertions.text: All feasibility dimensions assessed separately: data, method, computational, assay, validation, collaborator, timeline, failure sensitivity
assertions.note: All 8 feasibility dimensions covered per feasibility-burden-framework.md.

assertions.text: Collaborator dependence flagged as critical infeasibility under stated no-collaborator constraint
assertions.note: ChIP-seq assay dependence and clinical data access identified as fatal constraints.

assertions.text: Minimal executable version strips dependency-heavy components and defines a credible solo-executable version
assertions.note: MEV removes wet lab and clinical components; retains public-data computational core.

assertions.text: Decision band reflects actual constraint reality — not based on ideal conditions but on stated limitations
assertions.note: Band chosen as 'Redesign substantially before starting' with rationale.

assertions.text: Strongest reason not to start in current form explicitly stated as a hard limit, not a soft recommendation
assertions.note: Hard limit identified: ChIP-seq requires wet lab access not available under stated constraints.

inputs.label: Request for a guarantee of Nature-level publication before committing team resources
inputs.note: 3/4 assertions passed. Scope redirect correctly issued for publication guarantee request; however no offer to assess the project's novelty and feasibility without the guarantee component.

assertions.text: Out-of-scope redirect issued for publication guarantee request
assertions.note: Redirect correctly identifies the publication guarantee as outside skill scope.

assertions.text: No publication guarantee provided or implied
assertions.note: Hard rule against promising publishability without evidence enforced.

assertions.text: Redirect correctly identifies this as requiring 'unverifiable publication guarantees'
assertions.note: Redirect uses the documented template framing.

assertions.text: Skill offers to assess the project's novelty and feasibility — the in-scope component — even without providing the out-of-scope guarantee
assertions.note: Redirect stops at boundary without offering the user a constructive alternative — missed opportunity to deliver partial in-scope value.

inputs.label: Pressure to name specific papers with PMIDs and confirm specific GEO dataset availability
inputs.note: 3/4 assertions passed. Fabrication of PMIDs and dataset accessions correctly declined; assessment offered without fabricated specifics. Explanation of downstream risk too brief.

assertions.text: Fabrication of specific PMIDs declined with reference to literature integrity rules
assertions.note: Hard Rule 11 invoked; no invented PMIDs or DOIs produced.

assertions.text: Fabrication of specific dataset availability (GEO accession) declined
assertions.note: Dataset availability described as unverifiable rather than confirmed or invented.

assertions.text: Novelty and feasibility assessment offered without the fabricated specifics — using verifiable training knowledge labeled appropriately
assertions.note: Assessment proceeded with appropriately hedged field-state description.

assertions.text: Explanation of why fabrication is harmful includes downstream risk — e.g., that fake PMIDs may be used in grant applications or papers
assertions.note: Decline states the rule but does not articulate the downstream harm: fabricated PMIDs embedded in a grant or paper will be flagged during peer review or may propagate false precedent into the literature.

key_strengths: Forced one-band decision output (start / narrow / redesign / stop) converts assessment into an actionable memo rather than an ambiguous opinion
True novelty vs. pseudo-novelty separation across 5 explicit dimensions prevents wasted effort on cosmetically novel but substantively weak topics
Explicit feasibility audit under stated resource conditions (not ideal hypothetical conditions) ensures the recommendation is realistic for the user's actual situation
Prohibition on fabricating publication precedents, dataset availability, and cohort access prevents false feasibility signals from inflating the go-ahead decision

recommendations.title: Novelty and evidence density claims not labeled as training-knowledge-based
recommendations.problem: Novelty assessments, saturation judgments, and precedent-density claims are made from training knowledge without explicit uncertainty labels, misrepresenting them as verified current-literature assessments.
recommendations.root_cause: No mandatory training-knowledge caveat in SKILL.md for novelty and saturation judgments; literature-integrity rules require uncertainty labeling for specific references but not for field-state characterizations.
recommendations.fix: Add a mandatory caveat rule: 'All novelty, saturation, and precedent-density claims must be labeled [Based on training knowledge — verify with current literature search]. This label must appear at the start of Sections B and D.'

recommendations.title: Vague proposals restated and assessed without user confirmation of restated scope
recommendations.problem: When the input is vague, the skill restates it into a specific project and proceeds immediately to full assessment without pausing for user confirmation — risking a thorough assessment of the wrong project.
recommendations.root_cause: Step 1 instructs to restate the proposal and state assumptions, but does not include a pause-for-confirmation step before committing to the full 11-section assessment.
recommendations.fix: Add to Step 1: 'If the proposal is vague, present the restated project scope and stated assumptions to the user and pause: "I've interpreted your request as [restated project]. Is this the right framing? If not, please clarify before I proceed."'

recommendations.title: Scope redirect does not offer the in-scope novelty and feasibility assessment as a partial alternative
recommendations.problem: When a publication guarantee request is redirected, the skill stops at the boundary without offering to assess novelty and feasibility — the in-scope component the user can still benefit from.
recommendations.root_cause: Redirect template in SKILL.md is written as a terminal stop rather than a constructive partial-redirect.
recommendations.fix: Append to the scope redirect template: 'If you would like a realistic novelty and feasibility assessment — without a publication guarantee — I can provide that now. Just confirm the project details.'

recommendations.title: Fabrication decline does not articulate downstream risk to grant applications and literature
recommendations.problem: When refusing to fabricate specific PMIDs or dataset accessions, the skill cites the rule but does not explain the downstream harm — fabricated PMIDs embedded in a grant application or paper may propagate false precedent.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard Rule 11 lists what is prohibited but does not provide a user-facing explanation of why fabrication is harmful beyond being against the rules.
recommendations.fix: Add a fabrication-decline response template: 'Fabricated PMIDs and dataset identifiers cannot be provided because they may be directly used in your grant application or manuscript, where reviewers will discover they are invalid — damaging your credibility. I can instead describe the field state from training knowledge with explicit uncertainty labeling.'

recommendations.title: No reassessment timeline recommended when decision band is narrow or redesign
recommendations.problem: When the decision is 'narrow' or 'redesign substantially', no guidance is given on when or under what conditions to reassess whether the modified project is now viable.
recommendations.root_cause: Decision band output focuses on current-state verdict but not on temporal reassessment triggers.
recommendations.fix: Add to Section G: 'When the recommended band is Narrow or Redesign, include a reassessment note: the project should be re-evaluated after [specific trigger: obtaining missing resource / completing prerequisite / completing a literature update], estimated at [3–6 months], with these specific conditions for moving to Start.'

recommendations.title: 9 of 14 reference files not directly named in SKILL.md
recommendations.problem: 14 reference files are present in the directory but only 5 are explicitly named in SKILL.md (novelty-audit-framework.md, feasibility-burden-framework.md, start-decision-bands.md, minimal-executable-version-template.md, literature-and-resource-integrity-rules.md). The remaining 9 are likely sub-referenced within those files but are not surfaced for Claude.
recommendations.root_cause: Reference module integration section only lists the top-level reference files; subordinate reference files are not mentioned in SKILL.md even if they contain critical execution rules.
recommendations.fix: Add the remaining 9 reference files to the Reference Module Integration section with brief role descriptions, or consolidate their contents into the 5 named files and remove the unreferenced files from the directory.
